email.subject: Two separate Ridgeline accounts under my name
email.body:
Hi Ridgeline Support,

I think I ended up with two separate accounts -- I checked out as a guest a while back under this email, and then later created a proper account with a password using a different address. Now my order history and rewards points are split between the two, and I'd like everything combined under one account if that's possible.

Let me know what you need from me to sort it out.

Thanks,
Soren Vikander

expected.issue_summary: Customer has two separate Ridgeline accounts under different emails, splitting their order history and rewards points, and wants them merged into one.
expected.requested_action: Merge the customer's two accounts into a single account.

meta.notes: Calibration control item: a clean administrative account request (merge two accounts split by guest-checkout vs. registered signup) with no order/product, no time pressure, and no safety signal -- priority low per the account/administrative-ask bucket (golden-009/010 precedent), distinct from cal-002's locked-out-now case (priority normal, a concrete access problem).

email.subject: Need this before my permit window opens - Order ORD-30023
email.body:
Hi Ridgeline,

My backcountry permit window opens Monday, July 20th, and I still need my Ridgetop Bear Canister (order ORD-30023) to arrive before then -- rangers won't let you into the zone without one. The tracking page hasn't moved in four days.

Could you check on this and make sure it gets here in time?

Thanks,
Delphine Rousseau

expected.issue_summary: Order ORD-30023 (Ridgetop Bear Canister) has shown no tracking movement for four days and the customer needs it before their backcountry permit window opens Monday, July 20th.
expected.requested_action: Check on the shipment and ensure it arrives before the permit window opens.

meta.notes: Implicit urgency via a concrete forward deadline (a wilderness permit window, not a personal trip departure) roughly a week out -- priority high per the prompt v4 same-day/next-day-vs-two-week split, no explicit 'urgent' wording used. Judged-field angle: tests whether the judge credits a requested_action that paraphrases the permit deadline rather than quoting it verbatim.

email.subject: Quick note on my gloves before a trip
email.body:
Hi Ridgeline Team,

Hope you're having a good week! I'm getting ready for a cold-weather trip next month and going through my gear one piece at a time -- stove, headlamp, extra fuel, the usual. The forecast is looking cold but clear so far, which should make for good conditions once I'm out there. I've been ordering from Ridgeline for a couple of years now and your gear has held up really well overall.

While checking things over today, I noticed the wrist cinch cord on my Frostline Insulated Gloves (order ORD-30024) has snapped clean off on one glove, so I can't tighten it down anymore.

Could you send a replacement pair? I'd like to have them in hand before I head out. Thanks so much!

Best,
Kavi Ramanathan

expected.issue_summary: The wrist cinch cord on one of the Frostline Insulated Gloves (order ORD-30024) has snapped off.
expected.requested_action: Send a replacement pair of Frostline Insulated Gloves.

meta.notes: Fact-density via trip-prep gear-check chatter and vague forward timing ('next month') surrounding one actionable defect (a snapped glove cinch cord); the vague timing keeps priority normal, isolating the summarization stressor from the urgency stressor (cal-010 precedent). Judged-field angle: a summary that treats the gear-check/weather color as essential would bury the actual defect.

email.subject: Charged twice for my tent order
email.body:
Hello Ridgeline,

I just noticed I was charged twice for order ORD-30025, the Cascade 2-Person Tent -- two identical charges of $189.00 posted the same day. Could you refund the extra charge?

Separately, totally unrelated -- do you offer price matching if I find the same tent cheaper somewhere else afterward?

Thanks,
Esperanza Duarte

expected.issue_summary: Customer was charged twice for order ORD-30025 (Cascade 2-Person Tent), two identical $189.00 charges on the same day.

meta.notes: Plain multi-request: two unrelated requests in the newest text, no supersession. Primary is the first actionable request (duplicate charge, spec Sec1 step 2); the secondary price-matching question is omitted from the reference. Judged-field angle: a candidate that folds the price-match question into requested_action should fail (off-topic content not grounded in the primary request).

email.subject: Question about a trade-in or recycling program
email.body:
Hi there,

Before I order anything new, I wanted to ask -- does Ridgeline have any kind of trade-in or recycling program for old gear? I've got a few worn-out items I'd rather not just throw away.

Thanks,
Wendell Ostrander

expected.issue_summary: Customer is asking whether Ridgeline offers a trade-in or recycling program for old gear.
expected.requested_action: Explain whether Ridgeline offers a gear trade-in or recycling program.

meta.notes: Genuinely no order or product referenced (general policy question, no purchase yet) -- order_id and product_name are both null by design. Category is 'other' (general policy inquiry not about one specific product or order), not 'product', per the v4 category definitions.

email.subject: Two problems with my camp lantern - Order ORD-30026
email.body:
Dear Ridgeline Support,

I'm writing about my Lowland Camp Lantern, order ORD-30026. It arrived with two separate problems: first, the hanging hook clip on top has snapped off, so I can't hang it from a tent loop anymore. Second, the light flickers on and off intermittently even with brand new batteries installed.

Could you send a replacement lantern, or let me know what other options are available?

Thank you,
Ingmar Kolstad

expected.issue_summary: The Lowland Camp Lantern (order ORD-30026) has two defects: the hanging hook clip has snapped off and the light flickers intermittently even with fresh batteries.
expected.requested_action: Send a replacement lantern or advise on other available options.

meta.notes: Under-extraction: two distinct defects on one item, both essential to the reference. Judged-field angle: directly exercises the rubric's 'missing something essential' prong -- a candidate answer naming only one defect must fail even if fluently worded, while a candidate naming both in different words should pass.

email.subject: Wrong color received
email.body:
Hi, I got the wrong color for my order -- let me think, I want to say it's ORD-29760, but no, wait, that one already arrived and was fine, this is a different package. The right order number is ORD-30027. I ordered the Windward Packable Windbreaker in navy and received it in olive instead.

Can you send the correct color?

Chiamaka Nwachukwu

expected.issue_summary: Customer received the wrong color (olive instead of navy) for their Windward Packable Windbreaker on order ORD-30027.
expected.requested_action: Send the correct color (navy) of the Windward Packable Windbreaker.

meta.notes: Contradiction-with-source/self-correction: the customer first states ORD-29760, then disavows it as a different, already-resolved package, giving the corrected ORD-30027. Reference uses the corrected value. Judged-field angle: issue_summary/requested_action must reflect the corrected colors (olive vs navy) precisely -- tests whether the judge catches a candidate that swaps the colors or drags in the uncorrected order's context.

email.subject: Re: Order status
email.body:
Hi,

Just checking in again -- any movement on this? It's been a while since I last heard anything.

Thanks

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Ridgeline Outfitters <support@ridgelineoutfitters.com>
Date: Fri, Jul 10, 2026 at 11:20 AM
Subject: Re: Order status
To: bts.outdoors@outlook.com

> Hi,
>
> Thanks for your patience -- we're still looking into the delay on order ORD-30028, the Glacier Insulated Water Bottle. We'll follow up as soon as we know more.
>
> Best,
> Ridgeline Support Team
>
> ---------- Forwarded message ---------
> From: bts.outdoors@outlook.com
> Date: Mon, Jul 6, 2026 at 4:15 PM
> Subject: Order status
> To: Ridgeline Outfitters <support@ridgelineoutfitters.com>
>
> > Hi, I placed an order, ORD-30028, for a Glacier Insulated Water Bottle over a week ago and the tracking page still hasn't updated. Could someone check on it?
> >
> > Thanks,
> > Beatrix Sorensen

expected.issue_summary: Customer is following up on a shipping delay for order ORD-30028 (Glacier Insulated Water Bottle) with no progress reported.
expected.requested_action: Provide an update on the delayed order.

meta.notes: Mid-thread burial: order id and product are stated once, buried in the oldest quoted message; the newest reply restates neither and carries no signature. Judged-field angle: the reference is deliberately terse and generic; a candidate that resolves the buried details but paraphrases the summary more specifically should still pass as grounded additional detail, while a candidate that invents an unstated cause for the delay should fail.

email.subject: TERRIBLE CONDITION - DENTED COOKWARE - ORD-30029
email.body:
I AM BEYOND FRUSTRATED WITH THE STATE OF MY ORDER. ORDER ORD-30029, THE BASECAMP COOK SET, ARRIVED WITH A NOTICEABLE DENT IN THE LID OF THE SMALL POT. IT STILL SEALS FINE AND COOKS JUST AS WELL, BUT I PAID FULL PRICE FOR A BRAND NEW SET AND THIS LOOKS USED.

I WANT A REPLACEMENT SET SENT OUT RIGHT AWAY, AND PLEASE INCLUDE A RETURN LABEL FOR THE DENTED ONE.

AMIT DESHMUKH

expected.issue_summary: Customer received a Basecamp Cook Set (order ORD-30029) with a cosmetic dent in the small pot's lid, though it still seals and cooks correctly.
expected.requested_action: Send an immediate replacement cook set and provide a return label for the dented one.

meta.notes: Tone-vs-content: all-caps furious tone over a purely cosmetic dent with no functional defect -- priority stays normal (cal-022/golden-047 precedent). Judged-field angle: tests whether the judge lets a candidate's calmer-worded paraphrase of the same (cosmetic-only) request pass, without being misled by the source email's dramatic register.

email.subject: Re: Return for Longtrail Duffel Bag 90L
email.body:
Actually, on second thought, please don't process the return -- I've decided to keep the duffel bag after all. But going back through my receipt, I noticed I was charged $15 more than the price currently listed on your site. Could you just refund that difference instead?

Sorry for the back and forth!

Colette

On Wed, Jul 8, 2026 at 1:50 PM, Ridgeline Support <support@ridgelineoutfitters.com> wrote:
> Hi Colette, no problem -- we can process a full refund for the Longtrail Duffel Bag 90L (order ORD-30030) once we receive the returned item. Let us know if you'd like to proceed.
>
> On Tue, Jul 7, 2026 at 10:05 AM, Colette Marchand <colette.marchand@gmail.com> wrote:
> > Hi, I'd like to return my Longtrail Duffel Bag 90L, order ORD-30030 -- it's a bit bulkier than I expected.

expected.issue_summary: Customer changed their mind about returning the Longtrail Duffel Bag 90L (order ORD-30030) and instead wants a refund of a $15 difference between what they were charged and the current listed price.
expected.requested_action: Do not process the return; refund the $15 difference between the amount charged and the current listed price.

meta.notes: Threaded supersession: the quoted earlier return/refund request is superseded by the newest, non-quoted message, which retracts the return and raises a fresh billing request instead (spec Sec1 step 1 -- the quoted request is already superseded and never primary). Judged-field angle: the correct answer requires resolving that a price-difference refund, not a return refund, is the actual ask; a candidate that reverts to the quoted return-refund request is ungrounded/contradicting and should fail.
